updates_mode_header: Highlight where imagery has been updated or is planned to be updated.
status_filter_tooltip:
Published refers to imagery updates that were made available within the last year.

Pending refers to imagery updates that have not yet been published but will be soon.
category_filter_tooltip:
Metropolitan Updates
Source: Vivid Advanced imagery from Vantor.
High-resolution, up-to-date imagery focused on major metro and high-interest areas worldwide.

Regional Updates
Source: Vivid Standard imagery from Vantor.
Seamlessly blended high-resolution imagery offering consistent coverage across large regions.

Community Updates
Source: Aerial imagery contributed by the GIS User Community.
Recent, high-resolution aerial imagery contributed globally via the Esri's Community Maps Program to enhance the World Imagery basemap.
region_filter_tooltip: Selecting a specific region changes the map extent to only include the sites meeting all filter settings, category, published date, and region.
published_date_filter_tooltip:
The published date refers to the date when imagery was added to World Imagery.

The maximum timeframe for which updates can be accessed is within one year. Updates published more than one year prior to today’s date are not accessible.

Pending option will show imagery updates that have not yet been published but will be soon.
open_download_panel_button_tooltip: Choose a version from the list to export a tile package.
open_download_panel_button_tooltip_disab: Releases selected for tile export can be managed here.
open_save_webmap_button_tooltip: Save these releses in a new Web Map.
open_save_webmap_button_tooltip_disabled: Choose releses from the list to build a set of Wayback layers for use in a new Web Map.
no_regions_found: No regions found that match the selected filters.
toggle_save_webmap_panel: Toggle Open Save Web Map Panel
regions_cannot_be_fetched: Regions cannot be fetched at this time.

sign_in_with_org_account_prompt_updates_: You are signed in with an ArcGIS Online public account. <action>Sign in</action> with an ArcGIS organizational account to access this feature.

sign_in_with_org_account_prompt_download: You are signed in with an ArcGIS Online public account. <action>Sign in</action> with an ArcGIS organizational account to access Wayback Export features.
sign_in_prompt_save_webmap_panel: <action>Sign in</action> to your ArcGIS Online account first to save Wayback layers in a new Web Map.
arcgis_public_account: ArcGIS Public Account with limited functionality
user_of_public_account: {{username}} (An ArcGIS Public Account with limited functionality)

select_release_right_swipe: Select Release {{releaseDate}} for right swipe layer
remove_release_from_webmap: Remove this release from your ArcGIS Online web map
add_release_to_webmap: Add this release to an ArcGIS Online web map
no_privilege_message: You signed in using an account that does not have privilege to create content in your ArcGIS Online organization.
sign_in_again_prompt: Please <action>sign in</action> again using a different account.
show_only_local_changes: Show only versions with local changes

download_complete_message: Complete! Check browser downloads for the MP4 file.
wayport_export_instruction_4: This dialog can safely be closed while tile packages are being created.
wayback_export_description: Exported basemap tiles are intended for offline use in ArcGIS applications and <offline-app-link>offline applications</offline-app-link> built with an ArcGIS Runtime SDK in accordance with Esri's terms of use: <summary-link>View Summary</summary-link> and <terms-of-use-link>View Terms of Use</terms-of-use-link>.
about_what_description: Wayback is a digital archive, providing users with access to the different versions of <world-imagery-basemap-link>World Imagery</world-imagery-basemap-link> created over time. Each layer in the archive represents a snapshot of the entire World Imagery map, as it existed on the date it was published. Wayback currently provides access to all published versions of World Imagery, dating back to February 20, 2014. There is an ArcGIS Online item for every version which can be accessed directly from this app or within the <wayback-group-link>Wayback Imagery group</wayback-group-link>. The source code for this app is available on <github-repo-link>Github</github-repo-link>.
about_why_description: As World Imagery is updated with more current imagery, new versions of the map are published. When and where updates occur, the previous imagery is replaced and is no longer visible. For many use cases, the new imagery is more desirable and typically preferred. Other times, however, the previous imagery may support use cases that the new imagery does not. In these cases, a user may need to access a previous version of World Imagery.
about_how_description: Available versions of the World Imagery map are presented within a timeline and as layers in a list. Versions that resulted in local changes are highlighted in bold white, and the layer currently selected is highlighted in blue. Point and click on the map for additional imagery details within the selected layer. One or more layers can be added to a queue and pushed to a new ArcGIS Online web map.
no_metadata_found: No metadata found for this location.

metadata_resolution: Resolution: Pixels in the source image represent a ground distance of {{resolution}} meters.
metadata_accuracy: Accuracy: Objects displayed in this image are within {{accuracy}} meters of true location.
copy_coordinates: click to copy the coordinates of this location
